applicability.observability_scope_summary: verlyn13.github.io is a static site. No LLM is called at deploy-time, run-time, or by visitors. The only agentic activity is the OPERATOR's Claude Code / Cursor IDE sessions while editing — that LLM activity is hosted out of repo and produces no telemetry observable by this repo's audit. The §7 Phase 7 quartet (cost/latency/quality/provenance) is therefore largely not-applicable; the provenance split (§9.8) IS applicable because (a) the deployed HTML is generated content, (b) the CI/CD pipeline emits build/source provenance signals, (c) the PostToolUse hook (Phase 6 A-04) is an automation principal that emits no provenance.

telemetry_explicitly_forbidden.rule: AGENTS.md:112 — 'Never... Add external font loading, tracking scripts, or heavy dependencies'
telemetry_explicitly_forbidden.interpretation: Client-side observability is forbidden by policy. The site has 0 analytics scripts, 0 tracking pixels, 0 RUM endpoints. This is a deliberate choice (Phase 0 academic-credibility theme + AGENTS.md L121 'Academic credibility over commercial appeal. Clean, fast, respectful of visitor's time').

logging_surfaces.source: GitHub Actions runner stdout/stderr (3 jobs: quality, build, deploy)
logging_surfaces.kind: ANSI-terminal text via npm + biome + vite + actions/* stdout
logging_surfaces.destination: ephemeral GitHub Actions logs (accessible via `gh run view` or web UI for the workflow retention window — default 90 days)

citations.evidence: 3-job pipeline emits per-step stdout to runner logs

logging_surfaces.kind: deployment metadata (commit SHA, run ID, environment name, page_url)
logging_surfaces.destination: GitHub Deployments API + repo Environments UI
logging_surfaces.in_repo_emission_code: deployment is created by actions/deploy-pages@v4 (out-of-repo action emits the record)

logging_surfaces.source: local mise / npm / biome / vite stdout (dev workflow)
Claude Code session UI (operator's session log)
logging_surfaces.destination: Claude Code's own session storage (out of repo)
logging_surfaces.notes: PostToolUse hook (A-04) invocations are NOT recorded here — Claude Code records the Write/Edit tool call but the hook's bash subprocess output is silenced (2>/dev/null + exit 0). This is the single biggest observability gap.

limitations: GPG signing declared in AGENTS.md, not auditable from repo state alone (would require git log --show-signature on each commit)
no audit log of agent-vs-human authorship

audit_log_surrogates.surface: GitHub Actions run logs (per L-01 above)

limitations: records commit SHA + run ID; does NOT include SLSA attestation, dependency graph, or signed artifact hash

cost_latency_quality_provenance_quartet.applicability: the quartet is REQUIRED only for agentic and model-mediated paths. This repo has none in its deployed artifact.
cost_latency_quality_provenance_quartet.in_repo_paths_assessed: the CI/CD pipeline (3 jobs) + the PostToolUse hook (A-04) are the only automation surfaces; neither is model-mediated.

cost.rationale: no LLM call sites in product or pipeline; no token accounting required; the operator's Claude Code session cost is hosted-out-of-repo and unobservable here

latency.rationale: build latency is implicit in GitHub Actions logs but is not separately attributed per step in any structured form; PostToolUse hook has 10s timeout but no latency telemetry

quality.rationale: no evals exist; quality dimension is empty until Phase 9 records eval surfaces

provenance.rationale: see §3 provenance split below — only build-source partial; runtime-action absent; content absent

runtime_action.rationale: no agentic runtime in deployed artifact; no in-repo automation emits runtime/action provenance

PostToolUse_hook.cross_phase_finding: AAF-VGH-006 — silent errors + no log + no event + no trace = no provenance whatsoever for the single write-capable, auto-approved automation principal in the repo

CI_jobs.observation: GitHub Actions DOES emit per-step telemetry to its own backend (visible via `gh run view --log`) but this is not in-repo runtime-action provenance and is not consumed by any downstream system

content.rationale: the site does NOT advertise content credentials, content-authenticity manifests, or any kind of generated-content provenance on the deployed HTML. The site IS generated content (handwritten by operator + agents) but makes no provenance claim.

applicability_assessment.current_position: no explicit position taken; AGENTS.md does not address content provenance
applicability_assessment.is_this_a_gap: Yes, in the sense that a research-portfolio site COULD reasonably claim content authenticity (e.g., 'this CV is the canonical version, last edited by Jeffrey Johnson at <SHA>') but does not. Severity is low for an academic portfolio without high-stakes claims; severity rises if the site begins to host LLM-generated content where authenticity matters.

content.ff_candidate: FF-content-provenance-position-declared — every static site whose authors edit content via agents must take an explicit position (either: embed source-of-truth SHA in HTML comment, OR declare 'not-applicable' in governance docs)

build_source.rationale: GitHub Pages deployment record (L-02) embeds commit SHA + run ID in the deployment metadata via actions/deploy-pages@v4. This is BUILD-SOURCE PROVENANCE PARTIAL — it ties the deployed site to a commit, but does NOT include: (1) SLSA attestation, (2) dependency graph attestation, (3) signed artifact hash, (4) reproducibility evidence

evidence_present.what: deployment.commit_sha — tied to the run that produced the artifact
workflow run ID — embeds in Pages artifact
evidence_present.where: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 emits to Actions artifact store

evidence_present.auditability: DECLARED but not auditable from repo state alone; would require `git log --show-signature` on each commit OR a CI step that verifies signatures (no such step exists in deploy.yml)

evidence_absent: SLSA provenance attestation (would require an attestation step in CI; not present)
Signed artifact hash (Pages artifact is not signed)
Branch protection rules visible in-repo (AAF-VGH-011 declared-vs-enforced)
Reproducibility evidence (npm ci uses package-lock.json; package-lock.json is committed — this IS partial reproducibility evidence but not asserted as attestation)
build_source.current_position_classification: build-source: PARTIAL per §9.8 — commit SHA + run ID tie deployment to source, but no formal attestation; no signature verification; no SLSA
ff_candidates: FF-deploy-attestation — add a build attestation step to deploy.yml that emits SLSA provenance for the Pages artifact (GitHub provides actions/attest-build-provenance)
FF-gpg-verify-in-ci — add a CI step that verifies operator GPG signatures on commits merging to main (translates AGENTS.md:128 from declared convention to enforced gate)

semantic_convention_state.evidence: no OpenTelemetry imports anywhere; no semconv attributes emitted by any in-repo code; no MCP semconv applies because context7 is operator-installed (Phase 6 A-11) and not project-scoped

flags.context: PostToolUse hook (A-04) is the highlighted case; no automation in repo emits runtime/action provenance
flags.severity: moderate (because the hook is write-capable + auto-approved + silent — cross-cite Phase 6 AAF-VGH-006)

flags.context: site does not declare content credentials or an explicit 'not-applicable' position on content authenticity
flags.severity: low (academic portfolio; no LLM-generated content claims)

flags.context: this flag would apply if the build emitted NO source linkage. Here, partial linkage exists via Pages deployment record. Flag is downgraded to PARTIAL rather than absent.
flags.current_state: partial — commit SHA + run ID present; SLSA/attestation absent

flags.context: no evals to correlate (Phase 9 will record absence as separate finding)
no traces exist; no break possible
Phase 9 will record eval absence; Phase 7 records that even if evals existed, no trace fabric exists for correlation

cross_phase_ties.phase_3_E-12_to_phase_7: the PostToolUse hook is classified as event-triggered webhook with audit_attention=AAF-VGH-006 in Phase 3 E-12; in Phase 7 it is the single biggest no-runtime-action-provenance instance because it writes files but emits no log, no metric, no event, no exit-code signal
cross_phase_ties.phase_4_C-06_to_phase_7: contract C-06 (PostToolUse hook) carries runtime-provenance-missing flag in Phase 4; Phase 7 confirms the flag's empirical content
cross_phase_ties.phase_6_A-04_to_phase_7: principal A-04 (PostToolUse hook) carries ambient-authority + write-without-approval-policy + approval-not-enforced in Phase 6; Phase 7 adds the observability dimension — these flags compound to make the hook the single most concerning surface in the repo despite its narrow blast radius (file re-format only)
cross_phase_ties.phase_6_A-10_to_phase_7: principal A-10 (deploy job) carries the elevation+OIDC pattern; Phase 7 confirms partial build-source provenance via Pages deployment record but absence of SLSA attestation
cross_phase_ties.CI_static_analysis_to_dimension_11_7: the CI pipeline (biome lint + format-check + vite build) is a strong static-analysis enforcement surface (Phase 4 C-02 + Phase 3 E-04, E-06); Phase 7 §11.7 score will be ASYMMETRIC — good static-analysis enforcement, no telemetry surface

dimension_11_7_assessment.score_band: 1 (structured telemetry exists but naming and units inconsistent — applied charitably; could justify score 0 'logs only' since there is no semantic structure)
dimension_11_7_assessment.rationale: Score-1 reading: GitHub Actions logs + deployment record are structured-enough (per-step, per-deploy) and partial build-source provenance exists. Score-0 reading: no telemetry-as-product-of-the-repo exists; all signals are platform-emitted. Both are defensible. The asymmetry note (good static-analysis enforcement at dimension 11.11 fitness-functions vs. weak observability at 11.7) is the operationally important point.
dimension_11_7_assessment.exception_note: This score should be read with the applicability caveat: a static-site repo with no LLM runtime has a structurally lower ceiling on 11.7 than a model-mediated system. Phase 10 scoring should record this caveat explicitly rather than penalize the dimension on absolute terms.

ff_candidates_phase_7.rule: Every PostToolUse hook in .claude/settings.json must (1) tee stderr to .claude/logs/posttooluse.log, (2) propagate the underlying tool's exit code rather than 'exit 0' unconditionally, (3) emit a structured log line per invocation with timestamp + tool + file_path + exit_code
deploy.yml must include an attestation step that emits SLSA provenance for the Pages artifact (e.g., actions/attest-build-provenance@v1) before deploy-pages@v4 runs

ff_candidates_phase_7.ties_to: AGENTS.md:128 declared GPG signing → enforced gate
ff_candidates_phase_7.rule: CI must verify GPG signatures on commits being merged to main; AGENTS.md:128 convention becomes enforced (mismatched commits fail CI)
AGENTS.md must declare an explicit content-provenance position: either embed source-of-truth SHA in HTML comment OR declare 'not-applicable for academic-portfolio static content'

exit_check.exit_check_notes: All applicable §7 Phase 7 boundary declarations honored. The quartet IS explicitly scored as not-applicable for cost/quality and partial for latency/provenance, with cited gaps. Provenance split per §9.8 explicitly addressed: runtime-action=none, content=absent, build-source=partial. The PostToolUse hook is treated as the single biggest observability gap and cross-cited to Phase 3 + Phase 4 + Phase 6.
